title: Market Brief — Friday, June 5, 2026
summary: Compute is loosening fast across every GPU class. H100 remains the tightest at 12% util-avail (severe shortage), but A100 sits at 27%, H200 at 38%, and B200 at 42%, all easing sharply as 30-day utilization dropped 6 to 24 percentage points across the board. B200 spot fell to $6.24/hr median with the steepest utilization decline of negative 24.3pp, signaling new supply flooding the next-gen tier before demand catches up.
paragraphs: Compute is loosening fast across every GPU class. H100 remains the tightest at 12% util-avail (severe shortage), but A100 sits at 27%, H200 at 38%, and B200 at 42%, all easing sharply as 30-day utilization dropped 6 to 24 percentage points across the board. B200 spot fell to $6.24/hr median with the steepest utilization decline of negative 24.3pp, signaling new supply flooding the next-gen tier before demand catches up.

DeepSeek and open-source providers are burning cash at medium and high pricing tiers. Running Llama 70B on H100 at TCO-adjusted cost of $9.58/M tokens means DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $9.13 per million tokens, subsidizing adoption. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output and Anthropic Opus 4.6 at $25/M earn ultra-premium margins above 90%. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at 42% util-avail and 74% of offerings sitting idle is overvalued relative to current demand and will reprice lower. H100 at 12% util-avail remains the scarcest asset in the stack, undervalued at $2.62/hr spot versus its pricing power, so go long H100 capacity and short B200 exposure.
bullets: 12% util-avail on H100 makes it the only GPU class in severe shortage territory.
42% util-avail on B200 reflects oversupply after a 24.3 percentage point utilization drop in 30 days.
$80/M output for OpenAI o3 Pro is 3.2x the next most expensive model and commands 96% margin on A100.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.18/M output for open-source models undercuts the cheapest proprietary offering by 40%.
$3.95/hr H200 spot median dropped 1.3% day-over-day as utilization fell to 62.1%.
